body_txt: In contrast to the macho-ness and raw virility the Porn Stache tends to play into, a man who wears a pencil mustache is usually going for a look of suave sophistication or roguish derring-do, or wants to look dapper and well-groomed. Tuxedos, natty suits, stylish clothes and hats, and a clean-cut, well-groomed appearance usually go hand-in-hand with this style of mustache. It can be strongly associated with bachelorhood, as the mustache implies charm and a great deal of focus on appearance, the finer things, and personal grooming . This is meant to impress and attract women, as their possessions show they're well-off, and their grooming shows that they're clean and tidy. When paired with nice clothes, the mustache makes them appear elegant and pampered, almost promising that partners will be cosseted. Since facial hair in general is associated with a degree of steel in its wearer, men who wear this mustache will frequently be depicted as bold and surprisingly tough , especially if they're of the roguishly charming variant. Much like the horseshoe mustache , the pencil mustache carries some lesser-known LGBT associations. Coinciding with the mustache's association with bachelorhood, the term "confirmed bachelor" served as a euphemism for a gay man. The pencil mustache became part of queer coding because it signaled how they are fashion-conscious and flamboyant, traits that had long been stereotypically associated with the LGBT community. It also marked them as men of wealth and taste , indicating they are well-groomed, debonair, and attentive to their appearance. Such a mustache is not an indicator of moral character, as both heroes and villains will go for this look . If a Stage Magician is wearing a tuxedo and top hat, it's almost certain that he's going to be sporting this style of mustache. Subtrope of Manly Facial Hair . Compare to the Dastardly Whiplash , whose mustache is thin but very long and usually comes to points that extend off the face, all the better for twirling. Not to be confused with Upper Class 'Stache .
